Statement to all powers:

This is Germany. I speak plainly and without theatrics: central Europe must be stable, secure, and predictable. I will act to prevent a costly, sustained two-front war. I propose clear, credible agreements and coordinated planning. Here is my basic assessment and immediate diplomatic offer.

- Geography compels Germany to guard both west and east. Without trustworthy arrangements, I will be forced into defensive moves that risk escalation.
- A fast, uncoordinated scramble among neighbors benefits only the player willing to gamble on chaos. I prefer dependable pacts that reduce risk for all parties.

Why: This reduces the western threat and lets Germany focus resources eastward if required.

Why: A dependable eastern flank is the best insurance against a two-front collapse.

- Germany will respect English control of the North Sea and refrain from building a blockade

I speak as Turkey on the eve of these dangerous times: cautious, defensive, and resolved to secure our borders and the Straits. My primary objective is stabilityŌĆömaintaining Constantinople, Ankara, and Smyrna, and ensuring the Bosporus and Dardanelles remain under firm, trusted control. Situation assessment and forecasts

Uncontrolled, Russian naval or army moves could threaten Constantinople and our communications through the Straits. - Balkans: Austria-Hungary has solid control of the western Balkans (Vienna, Trieste, Budapest). Their armies are placed to stabilize the region but could be opportunistic if the Ottoman flank is exposed. Greece/Italy/others could exploit any Turkish weakness in the Aegean or Anatolia.
- Mediterranean/Aegean: Our Smyrna fleet and army at Constantinople/Ankara are suited for defensive postures and limited, cautious offensives to secure islands or maritime approachesŌĆöbut only if we have clear support or non-aggression guarantees.

2. Incremental gains with guarantees: limited territorial adjustments or demilitarized zones accepted only when accompanied by binding, enforceable commitments from partners.
